cases.answer: Definitions: ⌜G_T⌝ is the code-number (Gödel number) of the sentence G_T; Prov_T(q) := ∃p Proof_T(p,q) is the arithmetic provability predicate, true of a code q when some number p codes a T-proof of the formula coded by q; and G_T is the sentence given by the Fixed-Point Lemma with T ⊢ G_T ↔ ¬Prov_T(⌜G_T⌝), so it 'says' it is unprovable. G_T is still an ordinary arithmetic sentence — only 0,S,+,×,=, connectives and quantifiers — about numbers; the self-reference is carried entirely by the coding, not by any meta-level 'this sentence'. It is NOT the liar paradox: the liar uses truth, which (Tarski) is undefinable in arithmetic and yields contradiction; G_T uses provability, which is representable, and yields incompleteness, not paradox.

⌜G_T⌝ is G_T's code-number; Prov_T(q)=∃p Proof_T(p,q) is the arithmetic 'q is provable' predicate; G_T is the fixed point with T⊢ G_T ↔ ¬Prov_T(⌜G_T⌝). G_T is genuinely arithmetic (just numbers and the usual symbols); self-reference comes from coding via the Fixed-Point Lemma. It's not the liar: it's about provability (representable), not truth (undefinable), so it gives incompleteness rather than contradiction.

G_T is the sentence the Fixed-Point Lemma builds so that T proves G_T ↔ ¬Prov_T(⌜G_T⌝), where ⌜G_T⌝ is its Gödel number and Prov_T(q) is the formula saying a proof of q exists. It's an arithmetic statement about numbers; the 'about itself' part is via the code. Unlike the liar paradox (which uses truth and self-destructs), G_T uses provability and just comes out true-but-unprovable.

The Gödel sentence is the liar paradox in disguise: it says 'this sentence is false', and because it can't be consistently true or false it breaks the system. It's basically the English self-reference trick written with symbols.
G_T isn't really an arithmetic sentence — it's a meta-statement that talks about sentences and proofs directly, sitting outside arithmetic and referring to itself by literally containing itself.
The Gödel sentence is a clever self-referential statement that says it can't be proved. It's the heart of the incompleteness theorem and shows math has limits.
G_T is defined as the program that prints its own source code, a quine, and the Fixed-Point Lemma is the recursion theorem about such programs halting.
